Scenario.overview: Healthcare Organizations Simple FLORAHOX actors aim to disrupt healthcare services by exploiting publicly facing applications, conducting spear phishing campaigns, and exfiltrating sensitive data.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Active Directory, Scheduled Task, Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Custom Command and Control Protocol, Scheduled Task/Job, Command Execution (Mechanism), Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers exploit unpatched vulnerabilities in healthcare organizations' public-facing websites or applications.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers target patients with drive-by download attacks through compromised healthcare websites or advertisements.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers send targeted phishing emails to healthcare employees with malicious attachments or links.

stage.name: Execution
stage.technique_name: T1094: Custom Command and Control Protocol
stage.description: The attackers use compromised devices as Operational Relay Boxes (ORBs) to command and control malware.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers schedule tasks on compromised devices to maintain persistence and execute further attacks.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate sensitive patient data from compromised systems to sell on dark web marketplaces.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers launch DoS attacks on critical systems to disrupt healthcare services.

Scenario.summary: FLORAHOX actors conduct a series of attacks targeting healthcare organizations, starting with initial access through exploited public-facing applications, spear phishing, and drive-by downloads. They then establish persistence through scheduled tasks and command and control protocols before executing impact through automated exfiltration and denial-of-service attacks.
